title: SAP BASIS
learning_objectives: Understand the role of SAP BASIS.
Learn the responsibilities of a BASIS administrator.
Understand how BASIS supports all SAP modules.

introduction.title: What is SAP BASIS?
introduction.content: SAP BASIS (Business Application Software Integrated Solution) is the technical foundation of the SAP system. It manages the installation, configuration, administration, monitoring, security and performance of SAP applications. All SAP modules such as PP, MM, QM, SD and FI depend on SAP BASIS to function properly.

business_purpose.title: Why is SAP BASIS Used?
business_purpose.content: SAP BASIS ensures that the SAP system remains secure, available and performs efficiently. It manages users, system monitoring, database connectivity, backups and software updates while providing a stable environment for business operations.

major_components.name: Application Server
major_components.description: Executes SAP business processes and transactions.

major_components.name: Database
major_components.description: Stores all business and configuration data.

major_components.name: SAP GUI
major_components.description: Provides the interface through which users access SAP.

major_components.name: Transport System
major_components.description: Moves configuration and development changes between SAP systems.

system_landscape.purpose: Used for creating and testing new developments.
Used for testing before production deployment.
Live environment used by end users.

integration.purpose: Provides technical support for production planning.
Maintains system availability for sales operations.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: A new employee joins the organization. The SAP BASIS administrator creates the user account, assigns the required roles, provides system access and ensures the employee can use the necessary SAP modules securely.

best_practices: Assign users only the required roles.
Test transports before moving them to Production.
